// ZONE TEMPLATE: Copy this file to zones/<your-zone-id>.json and fill in all fields.
// REQUIRED FIELDS: id, version, meta, lore, rooms, bestiary, depths, dungeonLayout, boss, audio

meta.// name: Display name shown in UI (ALL CAPS recommended)
meta.name: YOUR ZONE NAME
meta.// tagline: Short evocative description shown on zone selection screen
meta.tagline: A short tagline for your zone.
meta.// element: Thematic element: water | fire | shadow | earth | void | etc.
meta.// colors: Primary UI color palette for this zone
meta.// mechanic: Optional unique mechanic label shown in UI (null if none)
meta.// unlockRequirement: Condition to unlock (null = always available). Future use.
meta.// emoji: Zone emoji displayed in lists and titles

// lore: Extended lore blurb shown on zone detail / loading screen
lore: Write your zone's lore here. Describe its history, atmosphere, and what awaits within.

rooms.// explore: Exploration room templates. Each template has a name and an array of variations.

variations.narrative: Narrative text describing what the player sees and experiences.

rooms.// combat: Combat room templates. Variation needs: id, narrative, enemy (creature name string).
rooms.// corpse: Corpse discovery templates. Variation needs: id, narrative, player_name ('{PLAYER}'), final_message ('{MESSAGE}').

variations.narrative: Narrative describing the discovered corpse and how their message is presented:

rooms.// cache: Cache/loot room templates. Variation needs: id, narrative (no enemy/options required).

variations.narrative: Narrative describing the cache room and its contents.

rooms.// exit: Exit room templates. Variation needs: id, narrative.

variations.narrative: Narrative for reaching the exit of the zone.

explore.// opening: Zone-specific first line. Pure sensory immersion. No plot, no enemy — just atmosphere.
opening: // Lead with what you feel/hear/smell before what you see. Ground the player in the body first.
// 1-2 sentences max. This sets the entire room's tone.
// 10 variations recommended for good variety across a run.
explore.// middle: The discovery, event, or tension beat. Something is wrong here — reveal it.
middle: // What you notice. Something that shouldn't be. A detail that makes the player uneasy.
// This is where the zone's specific dread lives. Make it concrete, not vague.
explore.// closing: Final line. The dread lands here. Short. Often a sentence fragment.
closing: // End on implication, not explanation. Let the player's imagination finish it.

combat.// opening: How the room presents itself before the enemy is visible. The moment before.
opening: // Build dread before the reveal. What does the space feel like when something is hunting?
combat.// middle: The enemy reveal or the moment tension breaks into action.
middle: // This is the turn — from tension to threat. Make the reveal land with weight.
combat.// closing: The last beat before the player must choose. Stakes are clear.
closing: // End just before the action. Player should feel urgency, not confusion.

corpse.// framing: Zone-flavored wrapper around the corpse. What does death look like in this specific place?
framing: // Death should feel native to the zone — not generic. A crypt corpse ≠ a jungle corpse.
corpse.// discoveryBeats: What you notice before you see the body. The environmental tell.
discoveryBeats: // The tell that something is wrong — before the player sees the corpse directly.
// A smell, a shadow, a sound, an absence. Zone-specific.

cache.// locationLines: Where the cache is. What the space looks like. Zone-specific detail.
locationLines: // The safe(ish) space. Describe it in a way unique to your zone's world.
cache.// toneClosers: How safety feels in this zone. It should never feel fully safe.
toneClosers: // The quiet is wrong. The rest is uneasy. That tension is the zone's personality.

exit.// opening: The first sign of escape. What does 'out' look like in this zone?
opening: // Not relief — recognition. The exit has its own character. Make it feel earned.
exit.// middle: The transition moment. What you leave behind.
middle: // The zone releases you — or lets you go. There's a difference.
exit.// closing: The last line before escape. Bittersweet. The hint you'll return.
closing: // The player should feel both relief and dread. They made it — but so did something else.

options.// cautious: Careful movement options. Flavor these to your zone's personality if you can.
cautious: // Zone-flavored if possible — 'wade carefully' fits water zones better than 'move carefully'.
options.// aggressive: Bold movement options. Urgency over caution.
aggressive: // High confidence, high risk. Match to the zone's combat tone.
options.// investigative: Examination options. The player slows down to look closer.
investigative: // These feed into lore discovery and corpse rooms.
options.// retreat: Hesitation options. The player second-guesses themselves.
retreat: // These can lead to alternate outcomes or just delay the inevitable.

bestiary.// shared: List creature names from the global BESTIARY to include in this zone (e.g. cross-zone creatures).
bestiary.// local: Zone-specific creatures defined here. Each needs: name, tier (1|2|3), health {min,max}, behaviors (IntentType[]), description, emoji.

local.name: Your Creature Name
local.description: A brief description of the creature and how it behaves.

local.// BOSS NOTE: Include your boss creature here with tier 3 and high health values.
local.name: Your Boss Name
local.description: The final guardian of this zone. None have passed.

// depths: Depth layers of the dungeon. Each depth covers a range of rooms and has a difficulty tier.

depths.name: DEPTH LAYER ONE
depths.description: The first section. Describe its atmosphere.

depths.name: DEPTH LAYER TWO
depths.description: The middle section. Harder. Describe what changes.

depths.name: DEPTH LAYER THREE
depths.description: The deepest layer. Describe the final challenge.

// dungeonLayout: Defines the sequence of room types the player encounters. totalRooms should match the structure array length + 1 (exit is appended automatically).

dungeonLayout.// structure: Ordered list of room slots. type: explore|combat|corpse|cache|exit. template: must match a template name in the rooms section above. boss: true only for the final combat room.

// boss: Name of the boss creature (must match a name in bestiary.local or bestiary.shared).
// audio: Audio paths for this zone. ambient.explore and ambient.combat are the looping background tracks. sfx paths are relative to /public/.
